page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. The CPU delivers top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Multi-core stress-ng performance is strong, while single-core performance remains average. Memory bandwidth is highly capable, reaching 7051.06 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 345.73 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations show strong to top-tier throughput, with Redis achieving 152.07 million operations per second.

For artificial intelligence workloads, the processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) support efficient LLM inference. Prompt processing speeds are strong across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds remain average. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM provides a balanced resource profile for large-scale deployments without the added cost of unused local storage. This instance is suited for enterprise databases, high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores, it features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data reveals top-tier multi-threaded performance, with exceptional bzip3 decompression, high-throughput static web serving, and strong Redis SET operations. Advanced instruction sets like AMX and AVX-512 accelerate cryptographic and mathematical workloads. This server is optimized for resource-intensive workloads such as enterprise databases, large-scale web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for high-throughput database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor for high-throughput enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine delivering balanced compute and memory performance for demanding enterprise databases and multi-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
Top-tier multi-threaded performance in compression, cryptography, and database operations
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated computing
Premium Storage capable configuration without bundled local storage or GPUs
Well-suited for enterprise databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based inference
